methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 2 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 3 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Archer Aviation
private.rationale: Same sector (Drones & Autonomous), same stage (Public), post-money $5B

data.narrative:
Joby Aviation is a publicly traded autonomous aircraft manufacturer in the Drones & Autonomous sector. The company has raised over $2 billion and carries a current valuation exceeding $10 billion. The comparable universe consists of six private peers—including Archer Aviation, Nuro, Elroy Air, Aurora Innovation, and ideaForge—with no direct public comparables in the narrowly defined sector.

Peer-median valuation analysis is not applicable to this assessment. The Drones & Autonomous sector contains only three same-sector peers, but filtering for consistent stage, adjacent-stage positioning, or similar valuation tier yields fewer than three datapoints in any subset. Applying an all-sector median would introduce material distortion given the heterogeneity of business models, technology readiness, and commercialization timelines across the broader peer set. This methodological constraint precludes triangulation via comparable company multiples.

The absence of both public peers and a sufficiently dense private comparable set limits the analytic toolkit available for independent valuation support. A defensible range would require additional methods—such as discounted cash flow analysis grounded in detailed production ramps and unit economics, or precedent transaction analysis if M&A data exists in the autonomous aircraft space. Without those inputs, this dataset does not support a quantified valuation range independent of the current $10 billion+ market price. Key uncertainties center on commercialization risk, regulatory certification timelines, and the sustainability of near-term pilot and demonstration contracts.
